turns.content: I thought using a regular HashMap would give me the insertion order I need for my game state, so I just wrapped it in serde_json::Value and assumed it preserves sequence like a vector.

That's a common assumption, but a standard HashMap does not guarantee insertion order because its internal hash table reorders keys on collisions or updates. If you need to preserve the exact sequence items were added in for your game entities, you should use the IndexMap crate instead of HashMap, as it keeps items in insertion order while still offering O(1) lookup by index.
Okay, so if I switch to IndexMap and implement IntoIterator for my custom entity type, will that let me iterate over them in the correct sequence without losing performance?

Yes, exactly. By implementing IntoIterator for your struct with IndexMap as its inner collection, you can iterate over entities in insertion order while maintaining O(1) access to any index via get_mut or get_by_index methods. This avoids the overhead of collecting into a Vec first and keeps memory usage lower than using BTreeMap when order matters.
